questions.q: Bose-Einstein statistics is named partly after which Indian physicist?
questions.explanation: The statistics grew out of Satyendra Nath Bose's 1924 work on light quanta, which he sent to Einstein.

questions.q: The class of particles that obey Bose-Einstein statistics is called what?
questions.explanation: Particles governed by Bose-Einstein statistics are named bosons in honour of S. N. Bose.

questions.q: Which famous physicist worked with Bose to extend his ideas, giving the statistics its hyphenated name?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein translated and extended Bose's paper, so the statistics carries both their names.

questions.q: In which decade did Bose carry out the work that led to Bose-Einstein statistics?
questions.explanation: Bose's key paper was written in 1924, in the 1920s, during the birth of quantum theory.

questions.q: The exotic state of matter predicted by this theory, formed when bosons cool to near absolute zero, is called what?
questions.explanation: At extremely low temperatures bosons can collapse into a single quantum state called a Bose-Einstein condensate.

questions.q: Bose-Einstein statistics describes the behaviour of particles in which branch of physics?
questions.explanation: It is a form of quantum statistics governing how identical quantum particles are distributed among energy states.

questions.q: Which everyday particle of light is an example of a boson?
questions.explanation: The photon, the quantum of light, is a boson, and Bose's original work was a new derivation of Planck's law for photons.

questions.q: Bose was a physicist from which country?
questions.explanation: Satyendra Nath Bose was an Indian physicist, born in Calcutta (now Kolkata).

questions.q: Unlike fermions, any number of bosons are allowed to occupy what?
questions.explanation: Bosons do not obey the Pauli exclusion principle, so many can crowd into the same quantum state.

questions.q: The two main types of quantum statistics are Bose-Einstein statistics and which other?
questions.explanation: Fermi-Dirac statistics governs fermions, complementing Bose-Einstein statistics for bosons.

questions.q: Bose's original 1924 paper gave a new derivation of which fundamental radiation law?
questions.explanation: Bose derived Planck's black-body radiation law purely from counting photon states, without classical electromagnetism.

questions.q: What is the defining property, related to intrinsic angular momentum, that makes a particle a boson?
questions.explanation: Bosons have integer spin (0, 1, 2, ...), whereas fermions have half-integer spin.

questions.q: When Bose's paper was rejected by a journal, what did he do that changed his career?
options: Sent it directly to Einstein in Germany
questions.explanation: Bose mailed his manuscript to Einstein, who recognised its importance, translated it and arranged publication.

questions.q: At which Indian university was Bose teaching when he produced his landmark work?
questions.explanation: Bose was a reader in physics at the University of Dhaka when he wrote his famous paper in 1924.

questions.q: Einstein extended Bose's photon statistics to predict the condensate for which kind of particles?
options: Massive atoms (a gas of bosonic atoms)
questions.explanation: Einstein applied Bose's method to a gas of massive bosonic atoms, predicting their condensation at low temperature.

questions.q: Bose-Einstein statistics helps explain which property of liquid helium-4 at very low temperatures?
questions.explanation: Helium-4 atoms are bosons, and their Bose-Einstein character underlies the superfluid flow seen below about 2.17 K.

questions.q: Which device that produces coherent light relies on bosons crowding into the same state?
questions.explanation: Lasers work because photons are bosons that readily pile into one mode, producing coherent stimulated emission.

questions.q: The first gaseous Bose-Einstein condensate was created in a laboratory in which year?
questions.explanation: Eric Cornell and Carl Wieman produced the first atomic Bose-Einstein condensate in 1995 using rubidium atoms.

questions.q: The 2001 Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded for achieving Bose-Einstein condensation. Which name did NOT win?
questions.explanation: The 2001 prize went to Cornell, Wieman and Ketterle; Bose had died in 1974 and never received a Nobel Prize.

questions.q: Bose's title in his original paper concerned the quanta of which entity?
options: Light (Planck's law and the light-quantum hypothesis)
questions.explanation: His paper, often titled on Planck's law and the light-quantum hypothesis, treated light quanta as identical particles.

questions.q: A famous boson, central to the Standard Model and confirmed at CERN in 2012, is named after which physicist?
questions.explanation: The Higgs boson, predicted by Peter Higgs and others, is a boson and so obeys Bose-Einstein statistics.

questions.q: Why did Bose never receive the Nobel Prize despite his foundational contribution?
options: He was nominated but never selected by the committee
questions.explanation: Bose was nominated several times but the Nobel committee never awarded him the prize, a frequently noted omission.

questions.q: What conceptual step in counting did Bose make that classical Maxwell-Boltzmann statistics did not?
options: He treated identical photons as indistinguishable
questions.explanation: Bose counted states by treating identical photons as fundamentally indistinguishable, a radical departure from classical counting.

questions.q: In the Bose-Einstein distribution, the average occupation number of a state contains which characteristic term in its denominator?
questions.explanation: The Bose-Einstein distribution has the form 1/(exp((E-mu)/kT) - 1), with a minus one distinguishing it from Fermi-Dirac's plus one.

questions.q: Which 2001 Nobel laureate independently produced a Bose-Einstein condensate using sodium atoms at MIT?
questions.explanation: Wolfgang Ketterle created a sodium condensate months after the rubidium result and shared the 2001 Nobel Prize.

questions.q: The link between integer spin and Bose-Einstein statistics is formalised by which physics theorem?
questions.explanation: The spin-statistics theorem proves that integer-spin particles must obey Bose-Einstein statistics.

questions.q: The wave function of a system of identical bosons has which symmetry under exchange of two particles?
questions.explanation: Bosonic wave functions are symmetric under particle exchange, while fermionic ones are antisymmetric.

questions.q: Apart from helium-4, the W and Z particles that carry the weak nuclear force are examples of what?
questions.explanation: The W and Z are spin-1 gauge bosons, force carriers that obey Bose-Einstein statistics.

questions.q: Composite particles such as the helium-4 atom behave as bosons because they contain what?
questions.explanation: Helium-4 has an even total of fermions (protons, neutrons, electrons), so their combined spin is integer and the atom is a boson.

questions.q: Which term, used in particle physics for whole classes of particles, was coined by Paul Dirac in honour of S. N. Bose?
questions.explanation: Paul Dirac coined the word boson, naming the entire family of integer-spin particles after Satyendra Nath Bose.
